restaurants.name: Song Fa Bak Kut Teh (JEM)
restaurants.notes: Classic peppery pork rib soup. Around $8–$10 per set. Comfort food that never misses.

restaurants.name: Kanada-Ya Ramen (JEM)
restaurants.notes: Premium tonkotsu ramen from Fukuoka. Rich broth, great noodles. Worth the ~$15–$20 spend.

restaurants.name: Soi Thai Soi Nice (JEM)
restaurants.notes: Authentic Thai flavours at JEM. Great tom yum and pad kra pao. Good for a relaxed group meal.

restaurants.name: Go-Ang Pratunam Chicken Rice (JEM)
restaurants.notes: Bangkok-style Hainanese chicken rice. Very affordable at $5.50–$6.50. Quick and satisfying.

restaurants.name: Shaburi & Kintan Buffet (JEM)
restaurants.notes: All-you-can-eat shabu shabu and yakiniku in 90 mins. Around $25–$35 per pax. Great group option.

restaurants.name: iSTEAKS Diner (JEM)
restaurants.notes: Upscale steaks at JEM. Great for a special dinner out. Book ahead on weekends.

restaurants.name: Ippudo (Westgate)
restaurants.notes: World-famous Hakata tonkotsu ramen. Creamy, rich broth. Classic Shiromaru or Akamaru from $13.50.

restaurants.name: 328 Katong Laksa (Westgate)
restaurants.notes: Iconic spicy coconut laksa brought west. Noodles cut short so you eat with a spoon. Under $6.

restaurants.name: Paradise Hotpot (Westgate)
restaurants.notes: HK-style hotpot buffet. Great variety of ingredients, decent broth options. ~$20–$30 per pax.

restaurants.name: Jjinja Chicken (Westgate)
restaurants.notes: Crispy Korean fried chicken. Good value at $8–$11. Great solo or sharing portion.

restaurants.name: Old Chengdu Sichuan Braised (Westgate)
restaurants.notes: Legit numbing Sichuan flavours. Braised dishes $3–$9. Good spice hit for a quick lunch.

restaurants.name: Blk 168 Woodlands Market & Food Centre
restaurants.notes: Neighbourhood hawker with solid local fare. Noodles, rice, and drinks all under $5. No frills.

restaurants.name: JW50 Hawker Heritage (Jurong West)
restaurants.notes: Blk 505 Jurong West. Michelin Bib Gourmand chwee kueh and chicken porridge. Old-school hawker gems.

restaurants.name: Keat Hong Food Centre (CCK)
restaurants.notes: 253 Choa Chu Kang Ave 1. 13 stalls, local and Asian fare from $3.50. Solid everyday hawker.

restaurants.name: 302 Foodhouse Bak Chor Mee (CCK)
restaurants.notes: 24-hour minced meat noodles near CCK MRT. Around $4.50. Great late-night option too.

restaurants.name: I Love Sukhothai (Limbang)
restaurants.notes: Authentic Thai at Limbang Shopping Centre. Great tom yum and green curry. Local favourite.

restaurants.name: Lot One Kopitiam Food Court
restaurants.notes: Air-conditioned food court inside Lot One mall. Convenient after CCK MRT. Wide variety of stalls.

restaurants.name: Takagi Ramen (YewTee Point)
restaurants.notes: 24-hour ramen at YewTee Point. Perfect for late nights. Decent tonkotsu for the price.

restaurants.name: Ah Boy Chicken Rice (V6 Food Court)
restaurants.notes: Classic poached chicken rice at the V6 Food Court near Yew Tee MRT. Affordable and reliable.

restaurants.name: Ming Kitchen Seafood Zi Char (Yew Tee)
restaurants.notes: Zi char at Yew Tee Square. Good salted egg dishes and cereal prawns. Order a few to share.

restaurants.name: Feng Zhen Lor Mee (Yew Tee)
restaurants.notes: Thick braised noodles with vinegar and garlic. Classic lor mee done well. Quick and filling.

restaurants.name: Sushiro (Causeway Point)
restaurants.notes: Japan's #1 conveyor belt sushi chain. Great quality at $3.50–$8.60 per plate. Always fresh.

restaurants.name: So Pho (Causeway Point)
restaurants.notes: Reliable Vietnamese pho and banh mi at Causeway Point. Light, clean flavours. Good solo option.

restaurants.name: Bali Thai (Causeway Point)
restaurants.notes: Indo-Thai with a relaxed dine-in vibe. Mee goreng, satay, and curries. Good for a group meal.

restaurants.name: Fish & Co. (Causeway Point)
restaurants.notes: Western seafood with generous platters. Great fish & chips and grilled options.

restaurants.name: Food Republic (Causeway Point)
restaurants.notes: Level 4 food court at Causeway Point. Wide variety of local and Asian stalls. Air-conditioned.

restaurants.name: Lau Pa Sat
restaurants.notes: Satay stalls at night are legendary. Great when you're in the city area.

restaurants.name: Maxwell Food Centre
restaurants.notes: Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice is a must. Queue early at peak lunch if you're in the CBD.

restaurants.name: Din Tai Fung (Orchard)
restaurants.notes: World-famous xiao long bao at ION Orchard. Book ahead for weekends. Special occasion pick.
